fix: split compound ptr+i32_to_u64 in substr/substr_len to prevent register clobbering

The expression copy_bytes_raw(ptr(s) + i32_to_u64(start), count) can cause
register clobbering on ARM64 when both ptr() and i32_to_u64() use the same
internal register (x9). Splitting into separate local variables ensures
correct behavior across all compiler versions.

commit.message:
feat: LUKS1 + LUKS2 full in-bootloader encryption support

Implements complete encrypted Linux drive support for UEFI targets:

Crypto layer (crypto/):
- SHA-1, SHA-256, SHA-512 hash functions
- HMAC-SHA{1,256,512}
- PBKDF2 (RFC 2898)
- Blake2b (RFC 7693) — required by Argon2
- Argon2id/Argon2i/Argon2d (RFC 9106) — LUKS2 KDF
- AES-128/256 block cipher (FIPS 197, table-based)
- XTS-AES and CBC-AES (plain/plain64/ESSIV) disk encryption modes

LUKS layer (luks/):
- LUKS1: header parse, PBKDF2 key derivation, AF-split key material
  decrypt, master key verification
- LUKS2: binary + JSON header parse, Argon2 and PBKDF2 KDF, AF merge,
  PBKDF2-SHA256 digest verification
- luks_vol: unified encrypted volume I/O, on-the-fly AES-XTS/CBC decrypt
- luks_open_efi: EFI_BLOCK_IO_PROTOCOL adapter for direct hardware access

UI:
- common/passphrase: masked passphrase input (shows *, backspace, ESC)

Integration (uefi_loader.c + ext4.c):
- encrypted=yes in sakuru.cfg triggers LUKS unlock before kernel load
- Passphrase prompt with configurable retry count (luks_tries, default 3)
- Optional luks_keyfile path support
- ext4_mount_luks: mounts ext4 directly on top of decrypted LUKS volume
- gBS_alloc_pool/gBS_free_pool helpers for Argon2 memory allocation

Config (common/config.h + config.c):
- New BootEntry fields: encrypted, luks_keyfile, luks_tries
- sakuru.cfg.example updated with encrypted entry example

BIOS target intentionally excluded (size constraints).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

instead of dir/./image.png. Bare filenames like image.png continue
to resolve as local to the markdown file's directory.

* update

* fix: simple mode inline #pause;/#meanwhile; no longer causes line breaks

In simple mode, the previous implementation split each text segment by \n,
added lines to result-lines, then joined with \n. When markers appeared
inline (e.g. "fn main() { #pause;println!(...); }"), this introduced
spurious newlines where none existed in the original source.

Fix: work directly with text strings. Visible segments are concatenated
as-is (preserving original newlines). Hidden segments with fill-empty-lines
use .replace(regex("[^\n]+"), "") to keep line count but erase content.

* fix(aggregator): use centralized document requirements service

Updates the FICA document aggregator to use the `document-requirements.service.ts`
instead of the deprecated `config/document-requirements.ts`. This ensures that the
aggregator validates against the same document requirements that were requested
by the Control Tower workflow (e.g., `BANK_STATEMENT_3_MONTH` instead of
`BANK_STATEMENT`), resolving the issue where workflows would get stuck waiting for
mismatched document types.

* fix(workflow): parallelise procurement and fica checks, and final approvals

- Refactor Stage 6 to wait for Risk Manager and Account Manager approvals in parallel, ensuring neither approval event is missed regardless of the order they are received.
- Add error logging for procurement check failures to ensure visibility without crashing the workflow immediately during testing.

* fix: resolve Stage 3 FICA gate deadlock caused by Inngest replay model

In Inngest, step.run() callbacks are NOT re-executed when a step result
is memoized (already completed). Setting context.documentsComplete = true
inside the 'mandate-verified' step callback meant this value was always
lost on replay, so Stage 3's ficaPromise always created a waitForEvent
for a second upload/fica.received event that never arrived.

Fix: return { documentsComplete: true } from the 'mandate-verified' step
and use mandateVerified.documentsComplete in Stage 3's ficaPromise gate.
Also replaced context.mandateType/mandateVolume (dead side effects inside
step callbacks) with mandateInfo.mandateType/mandateVolume from the
already-correct determine-mandate step return value.

Verified end-to-end: procurement CLEARED → Stage 3 FICA gate resolves
immediately via Promise.resolve (ficaSource: "stage2_documents_already_complete")
→ ITC/Sanctions/Reporter run → Stage 4 → human APPROVED → Stage 5.

Doxygen inheritance analysis: seeds + coverage gap map

Analyzed iccDEV class hierarchy from Doxygen SVG inheritance graphs
(inherit_graph_53.svg: 35+ CIccTag leaf classes, inherit_graph_39.svg:
17 CIccMpe leaf classes) and cross-referenced against fuzzer coverage.

Key findings:
- IccCmmSearch.cpp: 0% coverage (no fuzzer targets it)
- IccTagProfSeqId.cpp: 33% lines, 30% branches
- IccTagDict.cpp: 43% lines
- IccCmm.cpp: 47% lines, 37% branches
- IccMpeSpectral.cpp: 52% lines, 30% branches
- IccTagEmbedIcc.cpp: 55% lines, 36% branches

Seeds added (68 total across 5 fuzzers):
- 12 applyprofiles seeds: sRGB/Rec2020/Rec2100/Camera profiles with
  all BPS modes (8/16/32), photo modes (RGB/Lab/MinisBlack/MinisWhite),
  BPC, luminance, D2Bx, V5 subprofile, and embed-ICC flags
- 14 doxygen seeds × 4 fuzzers (profile/deep_dump/dump/toxml):
  NamedColor, SparseMatrixNamedColor, FluorescentNamedColor,
  MCS-CMYKOGP, MCS-6ChanSelect, ISO22028 Encoding (2 variants),
  SpecRef 6-channel (Input + Camera), ICS Rec2100 Part3,
  Calc 11-channel KubelkaMunk, Display GrayGSDF,
  Display Rec2020 Spectral, PCC CAM observer

All 68 seeds ASAN-clean. Applyprofiles fuzzer: 6589 edges from seeds.
Removed stray 'rec' file (LibFuzzer recommended dictionary dump).

Updated fuzzer-optimization.prompt.md with Doxygen coverage gap map
documenting CIccTag hierarchy, CIccMpe hierarchy, and core infrastructure
coverage status with improvement strategy.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Migrate database from SQL Server to SQLite

Architectural Change: Database Provider

This commit changes the data storage layer from SQL Server LocalDB to SQLite,
providing a lightweight, file-based database solution.

Changes:
- Replace Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.SqlServer with Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.Sqlite
- Update connection string from SQL Server format to SQLite file-based format
- Modify DbContext configuration to use UseSqlite() instead of UseSqlServer()
- Delete SQL Server migrations and create new SQLite migration
- Add System.IdentityModel.Tokens.Jwt to Infrastructure project

Benefits:
- Zero server configuration required
- File-based portability and easy backup/restore
- Cross-platform compatibility (Windows, Linux, macOS)
- Simpler deployment without external dependencies
- Still ACID-compliant with proper transaction support
- Easy migration path to SQL Server if needed in future

Documentation:
- Updated ARCHITECTURE.md with comprehensive SQLite documentation
- Documented advantages, limitations, and use cases
- Added backup/restore procedures and migration instructions
- Explained when to consider upgrading to SQL Server

Database File:
- Location: payslip4all.db (application root directory)
- Automatically created on first migration application
- Can be backed up as regular file

All components tested and building successfully.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
